tasks.title: Digit Sum Calculator
tasks.description:
When working with numbers, it is often useful to know the sum of all individual digits that make up that number. For example, the digits in 257 are 2, 5, and 7, which sum to 14. This operation appears in real-world contexts such as validating credit card numbers, checking divisibility rules, and analyzing numerical data.

Your program must read a single positive integer from stdin and calculate the sum of all its digits. Print the result as a single integer to stdout. The input will be a positive integer between 1 and 1,000,000 inclusive. If the input is a single-digit number, the output should be that digit itself.

WORKED EXAMPLE:
Input: 257 → digit sum: 2 → 7 → 14. Output: 14
